{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}

He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=draft}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=draft}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=draft}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=draft}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=draft}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=draft}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=draft}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=draft}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|12=draft}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=draft}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記|12=draft}}

He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=draft}}.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 臣狭山命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[日本武尊|倭武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[国摩大鹿島命]] | 子 = [[雷大臣命]] | 特記事項 = }}
'''臣狭山命'''（おみさやまのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 異表記として『[[常陸国風土記]]』に'''中臣巨狭山命'''（なかとみのおおさやまのみこと）、『[[続日本紀]]』には'''意美佐夜麻'''がある<ref>「桓武天皇 天応元年七月十六日条」『続日本紀』巻三十六。</ref>。
『常陸国風土記』[[鹿島郡 (茨城県)|香嶋郡]]条に[[鹿島神宮]]の御船祭の起源として登場する。同条によると、[[日本武尊|倭武天皇]]の御代に、[[武甕槌命|天之大神]]が臣狭山命に御舟を（もって天皇に）仕えるよう託宣し、臣狭山命はこれに従ったとされる。大神は夜が明けると「舟は海に置いた」と言い、臣狭山命がこれを探すと、舟は岡の上にあった。大神は「舟は岡の上に置いた」と言い、これを探すと今度は海に舟があった。このやり取りを繰り返す内に、臣狭山命はとうとう恐れ畏んで新たに長さ二丈余の舟三隻造らせて献上したとされる。
『続日本紀』では[[天御中主命]]の二十世・[[中臣烏賊津|伊賀津臣]]の父として登場する。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では津速魂命の十三世孫、[[天児屋根命]]の十世孫とされ、中臣酒人宿祢、中臣高良比連の祖とされる<ref>「河内国神別 天神 中臣高良比連」『新撰姓氏録』、弘仁六年。</ref><ref>「左京神別 天神 中臣酒人宿祢」『新撰姓氏録』、弘仁六年。</ref>。
[[狭山神社]]で配祀神として祀られる。
== 系譜 == 父は[[国摩大鹿島命]]であり、子に[[雷大臣命]]がいるとされる<ref>『[[尊卑分脈]]』</ref><ref>『松尾社家系図』（『続群書類従』巻第181所収）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[占部氏]] *[[風土記]]
[[Category:中臣氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おみさやまのみこと}} [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]]

'''臣狭山命'''（おみさやまのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == 異表記として『[[常陸国風土記]]』に'''中臣巨狭山命'''（なかとみのおおさやまのみこと）、『[[続日本紀]]』には'''意美佐夜麻'''がある<ref>「桓武天皇 天応元年七月十六日条」『続日本紀』巻三十六。</ref>。

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}} | children = {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Jingu|lt=Kashima Shrine|ja|鹿島神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine|qq=}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}}. His child is {{ill|Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonpi Bunmyaku|qq=}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Fudoki|ja|風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|qq=}}.

The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.

His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|ja|尊卑分脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonpi Bunmyaku|qq=}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]
