{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qid=Q55536814}}|father={{ill|Kuninazu no oukashima|qid=Q99517269}}|mother={{ill|Q108044745|qid=Q108044745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q99517230]] Wikidata name: Omisayama no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|qq=|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814}}|father={{ill|Kuninazu no oukashima|WD=Q99517269}}|mother={{ill|Q108044745|WD=Q108044745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q99517230]] Wikidata name: Omisayama no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]

During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>

'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814}}|father={{ill|Kuninazu no oukashima|WD=Q99517269}}|mother={{ill|Q108044745|WD=Q108044745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q99517230]] Wikidata name: Omisayama no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814}}|father={{ill|Kuninazu no oukashima|WD=Q99517269}}|mother={{ill|Q108044745|WD=Q108044745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q99517230]] Wikidata name: Omisayama no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | clan = [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], regarded as an ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814}}|father={{ill|Kuninazu no oukashima|WD=Q99517269}}|mother={{ill|Q108044745|WD=Q108044745}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no oukashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|WD=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|WD=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臣狭山命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814|1=Nakatomi no Ikatsu}}|father={{ill|Kuninazu no oukashima|WD=Q99517269|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto}}|mother={{ill|Q108044745|WD=Q108044745|1=Niikawa-hime no Mikoto}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Omi Sayama no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto, Omi Sayama | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}} | children = {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}} }}
'''Omi Sayama no Mikoto''' (臣狭山命) was an ancient Japanese noble from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, regarded as an ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}.
== Overview ==
The name appears with variations in historical texts, including '''Nakatomi no Ōsayama no Mikoto''' (中臣巨狭山命) in the *{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}* and '''Omi Sayama''' (意美佐夜麻) in the *{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}*.<ref>"Emperor Kanmu, July 16, 781," *Shoku Nihongi*, vol. 36.</ref>
In the *Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is associated with the origin of the sacred boat ritual of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} instructed Omi Sayama no Mikoto to prepare a sacred boat. Following the divine instructions, he constructed three new boats over two jō (about 6 meters) in length and offered them to the emperor. The text describes a mystical encounter in which the boat appeared alternately on land and sea, reinforcing his reverence for the deity.
In the *Shoku Nihongi*, Omi Sayama no Mikoto is mentioned as the father of {{ill|Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Nakatomi no Ikatsu).
The *{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}* records him as the thirteenth-generation descendant of Tsuhayatama no Mikoto and the tenth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, identifying him as the progenitor of the {{ill|Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja|中臣酒人宿祢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Sakado clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491611|1=Q135491611}} and {{ill|Nakatomi Takara clan|ja|中臣高良比連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Takara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Kawachi Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref><ref>"Sakyō Province, Kami-betsu, Tenjin Line," *Shinsen Shōjiroku*, 806 CE.</ref>
He is also enshrined as an auxiliary deity at {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|狭山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == Omi Sayama no Mikoto is identified as the son of {{ill|Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|ja|国摩大鹿島命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunimoro no Ōkashima no Mikoto|1=Kuninazu no Ōkashima no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99517269}}. His child is {{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Nakatomi no Ikatsu|lt=Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja|雷大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rai Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣烏賊津}}, who is noted as a significant ancestor in the Nakatomi lineage.<ref>*{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|WD=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}</ref><ref>Matsuo Shrine Genealogy (in *{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}*, vol. 181).</ref>
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|WD=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
